content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
spec: Add process_priority: str | None = None parameter to Editor.__init__ (stored as self.process_priority). Accepted values: None (default), 'low', 'below_normal', 'normal', 'above_normal', 'high'; any other value should be treated as None (ignored, no exception). When launching the editor subprocess (in edit_file/edit_files, wherever subprocess.Popen is called), after obtaining the Popen object (or via preexec_fn/creationflags at Popen call time), set the process priority: on Windows use ctypes to call SetPriorityClass on the process handle (win32process if available, else ctypes windll) mapping low->IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS, below_normal->BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, normal->NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, above_normal->ABOVE_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, high->HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS; on POSIX use os.setpriority(os.PRIO_PROCESS, pid, niceness) mapping low->10, below_normal->5, normal->0, above_normal->-5, high->-10. Wrap priority-setting in try/except (broad Exception, plus OSError/AttributeError/ImportError) and silently ignore failures (e.g. lack of permission for negative niceness, or platform not supporting it) -- editor must still launch successfully and edit_file/edit_files must not raise because of priority-setting failure. This must work correctly regardless of whether you rename edit_file to edit_files / support multiple filenames -- please apply priority-setting logic at whatever the final subprocess.Popen call site(s) become.
note: Editor.__init__ and subprocess launch site(s)

When launching the editor subprocess (in edit_file/edit_files, wherever subprocess.Popen is called), after obtaining the Popen object (or via preexec_fn/creationflags at Popen call time), set the process priority: on Windows use ctypes to call SetPriorityClass on the process handle (win32process if available, else ctypes windll) mapping low->IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS, below_normal->BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, normal->NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, above_normal->ABOVE_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, high->HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS; on POSIX use os.setpriority(os.PRIO_PROCESS, pid, niceness) mapping low->10, below_normal->5, normal->0, above_normal->-5, high->-10. Wrap priority-setting in try/except (broad Exception, plus OSError/AttributeError/ImportError) and silently ignore failures (e.g. lack of permission for negative niceness, or platform not supporting it) -- editor must still launch successfully and edit_file/edit_files must not raise because of priority-setting failure. This must work correctly regardless of whether you rename edit_file to edit_files / support multiple filenames -- please apply priority-setting logic at whatever the final subprocess.Popen call site(s) become.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/termui.py
spec: Add process_priority: str | None = None parameter to the edit() function signature in termui.py, placed after filename (or in a sensible position preserving backward compat as a keyword param). Update the docstring to document it: ':param process_priority: optional CPU priority for the editor process. One of None (default, normal priority), "low", "below_normal", "normal", "above_normal", "high". Invalid/unsupported values are ignored and the editor still launches at default priority.' Pass process_priority through to the Editor(...) constructor call inside edit(): Editor(editor=editor, env=env, require_save=require_save, extension=extension, process_priority=process_priority). No other behavior change to edit(); default None must be fully backward compatible.
note: edit() function only
